conditions: 6 AM-Well, well, well. We were certainly due for a snowstorm that overproduced on its promise, and yesterday's wild weather came through in the clutch. 9 inches of wind-driven pow plastered the upper slopes over the past 24 hours, with 3 of those sprinkled on after the lifts closed. The newly remodeled Rendezvous Mountain will meet Wednesday morning under mostly sunny skies that slowly transition to afternoon cloud cover, producing isolated snow showers. High temperatures will tickle the 20-degree mark outside the 10,400-foot Corbet's Cabin Waffle Factory and rise into the mid-30s down in Teton Village. Gusty winds from the west will be spinning the crystals you kick skyward as they crank in the 10-40 mph range. Who cares if it's the middle of the week? Succumb to the undeniable sunsation of getting lost in the glitterball flotation of a frosty face-shot's solar refraction. -Lege
